title: I showed a follower my $49k/mo faceless YouTube strategy

transcript.text: What is up vlog? Today is a very interesting day because I'm joined by Milan in our day in life of two faceless YouTubers actually and we have kind of interesting agenda because Milan has been running his YouTube channel for a very long time for 5 years and he also started few faceless channels and my goal for today is to help him start another one. >> You want to >> A very successful one and we'll be talking YouTube for the entire day. I've prepared a few a few events for for Milan. We'll go to the studio then we'll go to the cigar lounge. We'll basically talk YouTube the whole time and yeah, do you want to introduce yourself real quick just tell about your channel? >> Hi, I'm Milan and basically I'm doing YouTube for 6 years. Mostly I doing gaming niche for 4 and 1/2 years and now for 1 year I try to do something different and yeah, that's why how I reach [music] you to run it successfully. >> Nice. Try to run it successfully. I think you you you emailed me because you saw this this background and >> Yeah, from last videos I saw background and it was like familiar and then I little bit Google it pictures and yeah, definitely it was >> Funny. Funny because now we we're going to do a proper strategy session and yeah, we'll try to figure out. Tell me like how how did you end up in the place where like five of your channels failed? What niches did you try? What was that? >> I I tried history niche, history for sleep actually niche then I try POV stickman. >> Okay. >> Then I try one more uh, gaming niche uh, for a different game. And basically size comparison niche. So, those are niches that I tried be beside this gaming I'm doing as well. >> Makes sense. Uh, one of the things that I want to talk about today is basically how to choose niches based on saturation and how to, like, before you enter that, like, how to find out whether or not it will be worth it because I see, honestly, I see so many people, just like yourself, entering niches that don't serve their their goals and purposes. Uh, if you were to define, like, one goal for today, uh, for this conversation, what would that be? What would you like to to learn like the most? >> Uh, the most one phrase that I want you to teach me how to fish. >> How to fish? >> Yes, so I always have, uh, then idea of what could work and what could not work. So, I can all, like, instantly or in short period of time recognize is this good for me or not. >> Right. So, instead of giving a fish, we're going to give you a fishing [music] rod and we'll teach you how to fish. How to how to fish. >> uh, find profitable niche because, like, most of our conversation today is going to be about niches, honestly. Like, that's the like that's the bottom line. That's the main reason why so many people fail while others are succeeding. [music] It's always The difference is always in the niche and then all of the little details within that niche that that make, uh, that make a difference. Um, awesome. So, I guess with that said, we're going to, uh, go to the studio now and, uh, talk YouTube. >> [music] >> Awesome. That's good to know. >> to have you in the vlog. Let's, uh, let's get started. >> Cool. Let's go. Hey, real quick. Have you ever wondered how fast you could launch a faceless YouTube channel that you can sell as an asset for $30,000? Well, on July 17th, I'm hosting a three-day live event where I personally walk you through the entire process of launching and scaling a faceless YouTube business. Day [music] one, we're locking your niche using our cheat sheets, our niche bending tools, and a thousand pre-made niche ideas we sourced for [music] you. Day two, we find ideas and write the scripts for your first 10 videos, and you hire your first freelancer. Day three, [music] I help you turn it into a real asset, one that can be worth up to 20 acts its monthly revenue. The whole three-day event is called the Faceless Foundry, the first live challenge I've ever hosted, and I'm making it accessible for anyone. Tickets literally start at $67. If you want me to help you launch and scale a faceless YouTube channels for the cheapest it's >> [music] >> ever going to be, click the link in the description or scan this QR code on the screen, and lock in your ticket before we [music] raise the price. I'll see you there live. >> [music] [music] >> Milan, welcome to the first in-person strategy session, and I'm excited to break down your situation and figure out what is it that we can do starting this week to launch a brand new faceless YouTube channel for you. >> I start with a history for sleep niche, and basically it can hit as a most creator who start those niches with inauthentic content. >> Mhm. >> Even if I get like monetization and everything, then second, I try with POE history. And I didn't get so much traction. I think it was mostly because the competitors have for me, I think, better visuals and better like script writing. >> Okay. >> More engaging script writing, even if I tried to my best possible to have engaging script. I think that was the case, maybe not, but that was my opinion. >> Mhm. >> Uh then I try um an- another gaming um niche uh which is a micro facts, but it was getting really good. I get monetized, but um it have um a lot [music] of time I need to put in it, and while I'm doing uh two more channels besides that, competitors like post two videos a week, while I post one. What do you think is the big- biggest problem that you have in your business right now? >> Not necessarily a specific channel, but like overall in your portfolio. >> Niches, I think I choose the niche, I think at least 3 months I'm late. >> Yeah. >> against everyone. So >> You choose a little bit late, okay. >> Yeah, always choosing late when everything is at the peak. When it start like maybe to falling down, then I start with the niches. I'm like can't find the perfect niche before it's already right. >> So you you only enter niches when they are already getting saturated, and there are more players. >> I think that's the problem. >> Makes sense. So you're in a late wave of uh entering the niche. Yeah. All right, guys, and for for you in the vlog, before I start breaking down the entire picture for uh Milan, if you want to see that video with a uh breakdown of how I would scale uh his channel his new YouTube channel to $10,000 a month, um you can watch it on my channel. It's already live. Uh go check it out. Uh but we'll come back in a few hours when we finish that, and go enjoy the rest of the uh day. >> [music] [music] >> All right, so we uh finished wrapped up the recording. You know, how you how you feel? Was it good? >> Yeah, I feel amazing. I mean, a lot of questions that I haven't had now is clear and I have a clear path in my head. You know? >> Awesome. Awesome. Already finished the three amazing burgers. Now, smoking the cigar, going to reply to any questions Mimo might have as well and try to provide him with as much guidance as possible. And yeah, we'll probably also give a test to our new AI MCP tool. And then we'll see how the day goes. [music] >> [singing] >> What I want to ask one more question. How when you sell those channels? >> Yeah. >> You go through their website? >> Yes. Flippa is the best one. Yeah. >> And I mean, if I get to the stage, I will just ask you on Telegram. And how is deal later? Like somebody will just text you, "Hey, I want to buy your channel?" >> Yeah. So, basically, those platforms they act like middlemen as well. So, you have all of the conversations with buyers there as well. And then, typically, the way it goes is they send you the offer. If you accept the offer for, you know, you're comfortable with the with the money that they are offering, they transfer the money to the platform. >> Mhm. >> Then, you transfer the ownership of the channel to them. And then, once that it's done, the platform transfers money to you. So, this protects both sides, them and you as well. And yeah, everyone is everyone gets what they want. >> You prefer shorts or long form? >> They just serve different goals. And they're just different formats as well. So, with shorts, you can monetize them well on Snapchat, Facebook. With long form, you can get brand deals, you can monetize them on Facebook as well, but not like I haven't seen that many case studies with that. Um and you can do Spotify, but it's all about like what you do best, I think, because both work and both work pretty similarly. So, many people think that there's a huge difference between long form and short form. There's not. Like, the team-building skills are the same. like the way you find a niche is the same. Um like the algorithm is slightly different. The way you optimize your videos is slightly different, but everything else is pretty much universal. So, we run long form and short form channels as well. I don't see much difference in them, honestly. Well, I can't wait to see how how everything unwraps for you because uh now with the new niche strategy in mind, uh really interesting. Keep uh keep me updated on this. I'm quite curious. >> I will say to today or tomorrow to say the buy channel that proxy >> Yeah. >> and everything and then 7 days Now or I'll I'll give brother to list on >> Mhm. >> trust score and I will try to in those 7 days to find perfect >> [music] >> I mean not perfect, but niche that >> that worth it. Yeah, that is at the breakout stage, somewhere where like every single channel you see is getting views. That's the ideal uh case scenario. Like two to five channels, and all of them are getting views across the same format. That's how you know that the format is actually worth it. >> So, now that channel that you showed me, that's your main now channel? >> That's the one we started with a client, actually. Uh so, this is where I have the the stake, the like because we've been uh investing in some of our clients and helping them grow with our teams because what we do [clears throat] best is uh the team systems, AI, SOPs, all of that. And then what we're not a good as good at is day-to-day ideation, day-to-day scripting, and all of that. So, for example, if I see that my client is very passionate about the niche, he understands how to find ideas, write scripts, everything, it makes sense for us to partner. We will help him scale with the boring part, like hiring team members, you know, paying all the salaries, making sure all of the finances make sense, like all of the boring stuff. And then they will be the ones ideating and coming up with ideas and scripts. So, that's how we scaled this this channel up to 60K a month by now. >> Mhm. >> And it's been 7 months since we launched it, 8 months since we launched it. So, it's quite a new channel. I think the first video we posted the 17th of October, something like this, or 7th of October. So, like Yeah. 8 months. >> [music] [music] >> All right. So, Milan, tell me, you spent almost a day with me already. >> Yeah. >> So, how do you feel? What What was the most important lesson you learned about YouTube, and do you feel like this day is going to help you out in your in your journey? >> Yeah, definitely. First of all, thank you for having me. Um I'm definitely feeling motivated, and I feel I mean a lot clearer in my head how should I approach the new channel. >> Mhm. >> The most valuable lesson definitely was how to choose a niche, how to bend it, actually. I mean, that was kind of I mean, I watched your videos, kind of uh it wasn't clear in my head how it works, but now definitely it's so much clearer. And yeah, definitely pretty much a lot of valuables in this uh today day that I learned and most uh besides the most uh the YouTube niche bending is definitely uh those three steps you showed beginners. >> Yeah. >> The cash >> cash cow >> cash cow and that professional one that you say if we if we if we get >> [laughter] >> to a next level that we going to uh talk about that uh further and of course the selling channel also was mind-blowing for me cuz I really don't know about that. >> Mhm. Awesome. Well, I guess uh you pretty much have uh the same experiences many other faceless YouTubers at least like in what I see in the market. Everyone is kind of like starting the same cycle and uh the main problem there is that there's just not enough uh information about what's actually uh possible and how those niches are being created. And what we're trying to do with this with this plugs and even with those in-person meetings as well is just to share my experience on how we um niche bend and how we then structure the entire strategy and how we create those game plans because it's not enough to just think of okay, I'm cool and make 3K a month. Like I'm I'm good, right? You need to understand that your niche will day out, you need a new one, you need to have a strategy to go from a beginner to uh the stage where you're more stable, you have more uh consistency in your income and all of that. So, yeah, really appreciate you coming. I think that was a very good day. >> Yeah, it was awesome awesome day and uh full of lessons and as I said earlier, I don't know how thank you enough. I mean, this was pretty much amazing. Unbelievable. Still unbelievable. Like that I have opportunity to meet with you in person and go through different things how to make this happen. And now it's work time. Now it's time Yeah, now it's time to start working and hopefully to in few months we can make one more video how >> Yeah, once we >> how we succeed. >> [laughter] >> We definitely going to catch up. I'd like to to see how everything is going to unwrap for you and obviously see how you will be able to execute on all of it so that we can then talk more advanced strategy because niche selection is one thing but then once the niche works then the execution in that niche and the scale and season is really different and this is also something that you can't really find on YouTube. That's not advice that is out there. Something more advanced. So would love to do with a part two once once we get there. >> Definitely. Definitely. I'm down for it. >> Awesome. Well, then >> Appreciate you coming. Appreciate you having me.
